revision.comment: Created page with "'''Watanabe Kimitake''' (渡邉 公威; born 29th August, year unknown) is a Japanese businessman. He serves as the Eleventh President of Kanda-kai Ltd., Secretary-General of the Shineidō Head Office, and President of the Shinei Party. He was born in Shinjuku, Tokyo. He is known for having served three consecutive terms as President, during which he advanced the reorganisation of the corporation and the strengthening of its capital management framework. ---- == Overvie..."
revision.text:
'''Watanabe Kimitake''' (渡邉 公威; born 29th August, year unknown) is a Japanese businessman. He serves as the Eleventh President of Kanda-kai Ltd., Secretary-General of the Shineidō Head Office, and President of the Shinei Party. He was born in Shinjuku, Tokyo. He is known for having served three consecutive terms as President, during which he advanced the reorganisation of the corporation and the strengthening of its capital management framework. ---- == Overview == Watanabe Kimitake is known as the Eleventh President of Kanda-kai Ltd. Since assuming office, he has championed the centralisation of organisational governance and the establishment of a capital management framework, consolidating a system of administration centred upon the Ginza General Headquarters. His speeches and public pronouncements frequently evince a strong consciousness of governance, and he is equally noted for his leadership in times of crisis. His address delivered at the Ginza General Headquarters — ''"Our position is already on the verge of collapse. Yet it is precisely now that we must rise with indomitable resolve"'' — is widely known throughout the organisation. ---- == Career == * Born 29th August, year unknown, in Shinjuku, Tokyo * Joined Kanda-kai Ltd. * Active in organisational management and capital operations divisions * Appointed Eleventh President * Established his presidency for a third consecutive term ---- == Character == He possesses a keen and abiding interest in governance and organisational management, and his speeches are frequently marked by an emphasis upon the spiritual cohesion of the organisation. He is known for his candour in addressing difficult realities during times of crisis, and within the organisation he is sometimes characterised as a "President of resolute, commanding leadership." He has also demonstrated a thorough understanding of capital management and financial affairs, and is said to have been involved in the advancement of economic education and the development of investment frameworks within the organisation. Watanabe is a man of extraordinary industry. His physical constitution, tempered by the rigorous training of the Shineidō military, appears quite inexhaustible. In the early morning hours he subjects himself to a punishing programme of physical conditioning rivalling that of the special forces of the Security General Bureau; throughout the day he holds concurrently several positions — including the presidency of Kanda-kai — processing a ceaseless and formidable volume of affairs as they present themselves in rapid succession. Come evening, it is his custom either to discharge his duties for the Security General Bureau, or to repair to a distinguished bar in order to offer his personal attentions to those who serve under him. He is a man of exceedingly few words. When he dines with the equally taciturn Supreme Commander, not a single word passes between them; the only sound that pervades the room is the faint, delicate clicking of their chopsticks. Those who have had occasion to be present at such a table have since spoken of it thus: ''"It was so profoundly unnerving that one felt one's very years shortening."'' ''"One's breast was filled simultaneously with a reverence as though one had witnessed the most sacred of moments, and with a fear of unfathomable depth."'' ---- == Notable Pronouncements ==
''"Our position is already on the verge of collapse. Yet it is precisely now that we must rise with indomitable resolve."''
''"When an organisation is shaken to its foundations, what remains at the last is the spirit of its people."''
''"Governance is the art of establishing order."''
''"The loyalty we consecrate to the Supreme Commander is a bond that no surgery, however radical, could ever sever."''
---- == See Also == * Kanda-kai Ltd. * Ginza General Headquarters * Security General Bureau

revision.comment: Bot: categorize uncategorized page [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23359502324|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
'''Watanabe Kimitake''' (渡邉 公威; born 29th August, year unknown) is a Japanese businessman. He serves as the Eleventh President of Kanda-kai Ltd., Secretary-General of the Shineidō Head Office, and President of the Shinei Party. He was born in Shinjuku, Tokyo. He is known for having served three consecutive terms as President, during which he advanced the reorganisation of the corporation and the strengthening of its capital management framework. ---- == Overview == Watanabe Kimitake is known as the Eleventh President of Kanda-kai Ltd. Since assuming office, he has championed the centralisation of organisational governance and the establishment of a capital management framework, consolidating a system of administration centred upon the Ginza General Headquarters. His speeches and public pronouncements frequently evince a strong consciousness of governance, and he is equally noted for his leadership in times of crisis. His address delivered at the Ginza General Headquarters — ''"Our position is already on the verge of collapse. Yet it is precisely now that we must rise with indomitable resolve"'' — is widely known throughout the organisation. ---- == Career == * Born 29th August, year unknown, in Shinjuku, Tokyo * Joined Kanda-kai Ltd. * Active in organisational management and capital operations divisions * Appointed Eleventh President * Established his presidency for a third consecutive term ---- == Character == He possesses a keen and abiding interest in governance and organisational management, and his speeches are frequently marked by an emphasis upon the spiritual cohesion of the organisation. He is known for his candour in addressing difficult realities during times of crisis, and within the organisation he is sometimes characterised as a "President of resolute, commanding leadership." He has also demonstrated a thorough understanding of capital management and financial affairs, and is said to have been involved in the advancement of economic education and the development of investment frameworks within the organisation. Watanabe is a man of extraordinary industry. His physical constitution, tempered by the rigorous training of the Shineidō military, appears quite inexhaustible. In the early morning hours he subjects himself to a punishing programme of physical conditioning rivalling that of the special forces of the Security General Bureau; throughout the day he holds concurrently several positions — including the presidency of Kanda-kai — processing a ceaseless and formidable volume of affairs as they present themselves in rapid succession. Come evening, it is his custom either to discharge his duties for the Security General Bureau, or to repair to a distinguished bar in order to offer his personal attentions to those who serve under him. He is a man of exceedingly few words. When he dines with the equally taciturn Supreme Commander, not a single word passes between them; the only sound that pervades the room is the faint, delicate clicking of their chopsticks. Those who have had occasion to be present at such a table have since spoken of it thus: ''"It was so profoundly unnerving that one felt one's very years shortening."'' ''"One's breast was filled simultaneously with a reverence as though one had witnessed the most sacred of moments, and with a fear of unfathomable depth."'' ---- == Notable Pronouncements ==
''"Our position is already on the verge of collapse. Yet it is precisely now that we must rise with indomitable resolve."''
''"When an organisation is shaken to its foundations, what remains at the last is the spirit of its people."''
''"Governance is the art of establishing order."''
''"The loyalty we consecrate to the Supreme Commander is a bond that no surgery, however radical, could ever sever."''
---- == See Also == * Kanda-kai Ltd. * Ginza General Headquarters * Security General Bureau [[Category:Uncategorized pages]]

revision.comment: Bot: tag page without wikidata link [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
'''Watanabe Kimitake''' (渡邉 公威; born 29th August, year unknown) is a Japanese businessman. He serves as the Eleventh President of Kanda-kai Ltd., Secretary-General of the Shineidō Head Office, and President of the Shinei Party. He was born in Shinjuku, Tokyo. He is known for having served three consecutive terms as President, during which he advanced the reorganisation of the corporation and the strengthening of its capital management framework. ---- == Overview == Watanabe Kimitake is known as the Eleventh President of Kanda-kai Ltd. Since assuming office, he has championed the centralisation of organisational governance and the establishment of a capital management framework, consolidating a system of administration centred upon the Ginza General Headquarters. His speeches and public pronouncements frequently evince a strong consciousness of governance, and he is equally noted for his leadership in times of crisis. His address delivered at the Ginza General Headquarters — ''"Our position is already on the verge of collapse. Yet it is precisely now that we must rise with indomitable resolve"'' — is widely known throughout the organisation. ---- == Career == * Born 29th August, year unknown, in Shinjuku, Tokyo * Joined Kanda-kai Ltd. * Active in organisational management and capital operations divisions * Appointed Eleventh President * Established his presidency for a third consecutive term ---- == Character == He possesses a keen and abiding interest in governance and organisational management, and his speeches are frequently marked by an emphasis upon the spiritual cohesion of the organisation. He is known for his candour in addressing difficult realities during times of crisis, and within the organisation he is sometimes characterised as a "President of resolute, commanding leadership." He has also demonstrated a thorough understanding of capital management and financial affairs, and is said to have been involved in the advancement of economic education and the development of investment frameworks within the organisation. Watanabe is a man of extraordinary industry. His physical constitution, tempered by the rigorous training of the Shineidō military, appears quite inexhaustible. In the early morning hours he subjects himself to a punishing programme of physical conditioning rivalling that of the special forces of the Security General Bureau; throughout the day he holds concurrently several positions — including the presidency of Kanda-kai — processing a ceaseless and formidable volume of affairs as they present themselves in rapid succession. Come evening, it is his custom either to discharge his duties for the Security General Bureau, or to repair to a distinguished bar in order to offer his personal attentions to those who serve under him. He is a man of exceedingly few words. When he dines with the equally taciturn Supreme Commander, not a single word passes between them; the only sound that pervades the room is the faint, delicate clicking of their chopsticks. Those who have had occasion to be present at such a table have since spoken of it thus: ''"It was so profoundly unnerving that one felt one's very years shortening."'' ''"One's breast was filled simultaneously with a reverence as though one had witnessed the most sacred of moments, and with a fear of unfathomable depth."'' ---- == Notable Pronouncements ==
''"Our position is already on the verge of collapse. Yet it is precisely now that we must rise with indomitable resolve."''
''"When an organisation is shaken to its foundations, what remains at the last is the spirit of its people."''
''"Governance is the art of establishing order."''
''"The loyalty we consecrate to the Supreme Commander is a bond that no surgery, however radical, could ever sever."''
---- == See Also == * Kanda-kai Ltd. * Ginza General Headquarters * Security General Bureau [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Pages without wikidata]]
